source.title: Intervju tjedna, Filip Šaravanja - suosnivač Pazara

summary.title_hr: Intervju tjedna, Filip Šaravanja - suosnivač Pazara
summary.abstract_hr: Intervju s Filipom Šaravanjom analizira utjecaj Trumpovih meme tokena na kripto tržište i etičke implikacije, uključujući potencijal za korupciju. Razgovor se dotiče i regulatornog okvira u SAD-u, uloge SEC-a i Garyja Genslera, te kritički ocjenjuje europsku MiCA regulativu. Također se raspravlja o prisutnosti retail investitora, budućnosti kripto tržišta i prognozama cijena Bitcoina, ističući transparentnost blockchaina naspram tradicionalnih financija.
key_points: Trumpov meme token je bez presedana digao prašinu na tržištu, ali otvara pitanja etike, morala i potencijalne korupcije, iako blockchain omogućuje transparentniji uvid u transakcije.
MiCA regulativa u Europi je opisana kao 'Frankenstein od regulative' zbog tehničkih grešaka, nejasnoća oko DeFi-ja i NFT-ova, te stvaranja pravne nesigurnosti umjesto harmoniziranog tržišta.
Gary Gensler je, svjesno ili nesvjesno, učinio kripto u Americi političkim pitanjem, tjerajući industriju na političko djelovanje protiv stranke koja ga je postavila.
Očekuje se da će nova američka administracija donijeti liberalizaciju kripto tržišta, što bi moglo privući projekte iz Europe i stvoriti 'zamku' za europska regulirana tržišta.
Retail investitori su prisutni na kripto tržištu, ali je njihov utjecaj teže uočiti zbog veće buke, narativa i broja tokena na različitim blockchainima.
Prognoze cijena Bitcoina su neizvjesne, ali se očekuje još jedan do dva 'lega' rasta u trenutnom ciklusu, bez uvjerenja u superciklus, ali i bez vjerovanja da je ovo zadnji ciklus.

Kripto industrija se razvija kao globalni sustav za istinsku namjeru, omogućujući monetizaciju i lakši prijenos digitaliziranih vrijednosti, s potencijalom za bolju privatnost kroz tehnologije poput zero-knowledge proofa.
summary.title_en: Interview of the week, Filip Šaravanja - co-founder of Pazar
summary.abstract_en: An interview with Filip Šaravanja analyzes the impact of Trump's meme tokens on the crypto market and ethical implications, including the potential for corruption. The conversation also touches upon the regulatory framework in the USA, the role of the SEC and Gary Gensler, and critically evaluates the European MiCA regulation. It also discusses the presence of retail investors, the future of the crypto market, and Bitcoin price forecasts, highlighting the transparency of blockchain versus traditional finance.
key_topics_en: conflict of interest in the crypto world
role of SEC and Gary Gensler
key_points_en: Trump's meme token has unprecedentedly stirred up dust on the market, but it opens questions of ethics, morality, and potential corruption, although blockchain enables a more transparent insight into transactions.

The MiCA regulation in Europe has been described as 'Frankenstein of regulation' due to technical errors, ambiguities regarding DeFi and NFTs, and the creation of legal uncertainty instead of a harmonized market.
Gary Gensler has, consciously or unconsciously, made crypto in America a political issue, forcing the industry into political action against the party that appointed him.
It is expected that the new American administration will bring liberalization of the crypto market, which could attract projects from Europe and create a 'trap' for European regulated markets.
Retail investors are present on the crypto market, but their influence is harder to notice due to greater noise, narratives, and the number of tokens on different blockchains.

Bitcoin price predictions are uncertain, but one to two more 'legs' of growth are expected in the current cycle, without conviction in a supercycle, but also without believing that this is the last cycle.

The crypto industry is developing as a global system for a true intention, enabling monetization and easier transfer of digitized values, with the potential for better privacy through technologies like zero-knowledge proof.
